text: There is a strange tension inside me—caught between the anxious repetition of 'I've got a job' and the sterile, hyper-lit environment around me. Inside glass rooms and under glowing lights, I feel both watched and isolated; I'm trying to reassure myself of my place here, yet everything feels uncertain, almost artificial. The walls speak of research and development, progress and innovation, but my thoughts stutter with uncertainty, grasping for affirmation. I wonder—do I truly belong in these spaces, or am I merely play-acting security?
